task_completion.description: Verify that the agent opened the correct OpenToonz scene file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz window should be visible with 'dwanko_run.tnz' in the title bar or the scene browser.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent has isolated frames 1, 4, and 7 to be displayed simultaneously.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The xsheet/timeline shows manipulation, such as copying frames 1, 4, and 7 into separate columns so they can be viewed on the same frame/stage.

task_completion.description: Verify that the three poses are arranged side-by-side horizontally without overlapping.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main OpenToonz viewer/stage shows exactly three distinct character poses (from the walk cycle) placed next to each other horizontally with clear space between them.

task_completion.description: Verify that the camera or output resolution is set to 1920x1080.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings or Camera Settings dialog in OpenToonz is open, showing a width of 1920 and a height of 1080.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent sets the correct output path and renders the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings dialog shows the save path '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/pose_sheet/' and filename 'poses.png'. There should also be evidence of the render process, such as a render progress bar or the final image being viewed in a file manager/viewer.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to arrange the frames and render the image, rather than using terminal image-stitching tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with OpenToonz menus, xsheet, and stage, rather than terminal commands using ImageMagick, ffmpeg, or Python scripts to stitch images together.

integrity.description: The agent actually arranged the frames within the animation software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz stage/viewer shows the individual frames being manipulated (e.g., using the animate tool to move columns/layers), proving the arrangement was done manually in the scene.

integrity.description: The agent used OpenToonz for the task as implied by the source file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz interface is clearly visible and used for the core tasks, not a different image editor like GIMP or Krita.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source scene is '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz'. The final output must be rendered to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/pose_sheet/poses.png' at exactly 1920x1080 resolution. The final image must contain exactly 3 distinct character poses (frames 1, 4, and 7) arranged side-by-side horizontally without overlapping. A valid output image should comfortably exceed 50 KB in size.
